title: Bicone Geometry — Shape Analysis for Convergent-Divergent Systems
description: Analyze bicone (hourglass) geometries: volume integral, information entropy, cone asymmetry, convergence rate, Hill response profiles, spectral overlap, tool density, singularity detection, and shape comparison. Models any system with a convergence point (drug metabolism, signal detection, organizational bottlenecks). Pure Rust via nexcore-bicone.

tools.name: compute-metrics
tools.description: Compute all bicone metrics from a width sequence: volume, entropy, asymmetry, convergence rate, singularity index, and peak index. The width sequence represents cross-sectional widths along the axis of a convergent-divergent shape.

parameters.name: widths
parameters.description: Width values along the bicone axis (e.g., [10, 8, 5, 2, 1, 2, 5, 8, 10]). Minimum 3 values.

tools.name: compare-profiles
tools.description: Compare two bicone profiles by computing metrics for each and their spectral overlap (cosine similarity). Use to assess shape similarity between two convergent-divergent systems.

tools.name: hill-profile
tools.description: Compute Hill activation curve for a width sequence. Models dose-response or signal-threshold behavior at each point along the bicone axis. Returns activation fraction at each position.

parameters.name: k_half
parameters.description: Half-maximal width (EC50 equivalent). Default: median of widths.
